courses.subject: RMCE
courses.title: Basic Clinical Embryology

meetings.instructor:
Professor CHAN Yiu Leung, 
Professor CHUNG Pui Wah Jacqueline

courses.description:
The aim of this module is to provide basic knowledge on human embryology from fertilization through birth. It includes:
1. The mammalian reproductive system
2. Gametogenesis
3. Fertilisation and Early embryogenesis
4. Embryonic Development of special systems
5. Congenital malformations
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Reproductive Medicine and Clinical Embryology
courses.course_syllabus: The topics will be stated in the course timetable and it will be provided in the first lesson.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Collection by paper evaluation form at last lesson
courses.required_readings: The required reading will be provided in the first lesson.
courses.recommended_readings: The recommended reading will be provided in the first lesson,

courses.subject: RMCE
courses.title: Andrology

meetings.instructor:
Professor CHAN Yiu Leung, 
Professor CHUNG Pui Wah Jacqueline

courses.description:
This course provides a knowledge set appropriate for the broad range of health care professionals involved in the treatment of the infertile male, provide training in the assessement of parameters of male reproductive function, and assist professionals in identifying strategies for the treatment of male infertility. The course includes:
1. The physiology and pathophysiology of male reproductive function
2. Key elements of the male factor evaluation
3. Basic and advanced testing of reproductive function
4. Surgical and nonsurgical treatments of male infertility
5. Strategies for male fertility preservation
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Reproductive Medicine and Clinical Embryology

courses.subject: RMCE
courses.title: Reproductive Medicine & Assisted Reproductive Technology – Overview

meetings.instructor:
Professor CHAN Yiu Leung, 
Professor CHUNG Pui Wah Jacqueline

courses.description:
This course allows students to gain an overall picture of ART, including basic conception, definition, basic clinical procedure, basic lab procedure, as well as the most updated knowledge and technology. It includes:
1. The development of ART history
2. The components of ART team
3. The basic procedures and responsibility of each ART components
4. Communication of each components
5. Definition and diagnosis of infertility
6. Treatment strategies of infertility
7. Patient support and counselling

Assessment: written examination
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Reproductive Medicine and Clinical Embryology
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students are expected to:
1. Understand the ART history and current technology development
2. Acquire the basic conception of Reproductive medicine and ART
3. Acquire basic knowledge on the ART treatment
4. Understand the whole procedure of ART in current clinical practice
courses.course_syllabus:
The development of ART history
The components of ART team
The basic procedures and responsibility of each ART components
Communication of each components
Definition and diagnosis of infertility
Treatment strategies of infertility
Patient support and counselling
courses.feedback_evaluation: Collection by paper evaluation form at last lesson
courses.required_readings: The required readings will be provided in the first lesson.
courses.recommended_readings: The recommended readings will be provided in the first lesson.

courses.subject: RMCE
courses.title: Reproductive Medicine & Assisted Reproductive Technology - Clinical

meetings.instructor:
Professor CHAN Yiu Leung, 
Professor CHUNG Pui Wah Jacqueline

courses.description:
This course allows students to gain detail knowledge and basic information about the modern ART treatment, infertility complex issues, as well as current management strategies for infertile patients.
It includes:
1. Investigation of infertility
2. Treatment strategies of infertility
3. Ovulation induction
4. In vitro fertilization in a clinical IVF lab
5. Complications of ART (e.g. ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome)
6. Counseling of infertile complex issues
7. Patient support
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Reproductive Medicine and Clinical Embryology
courses.learning_outcomes:
Learning outcome
The students are expected to have knowledge and understand:
1. Infertility investigation
2. Infertility diagnosis
3. Infertility treatment strategies
4. The principle and strategy of ovulation induction
5. Basic procedure of oocyte retrieval
6. Basic procedure of embryo transfer
7. The strategy in counseling infertile patients
8. How to handle complications of ART treatment in clinical
courses.course_syllabus:
Infertility investigation
Infertility diagnosis
Infertility treatment strategies
The principle and strategy of ovulation induction
Basic procedure of oocyte retrieval
Basic procedure of embryo transfer
The strategy in counseling infertile patients
How to handle complications of ART treatment in clinical
courses.feedback_evaluation: Collection by paper evaluation form at last lesson

courses.subject: RMCE
courses.title: Cell Biology and Molecular Biology

meetings.instructor:
Professor CHAN Yiu Leung, 
Professor CHUNG Pui Wah Jacqueline

courses.description:
The aim of this module is to quickly review the reproductive medicine related basic knowledge of cell biology and molecular biology, as well as the most update related knowledge. It includes:
1. The cell structure and cell organelles
2. The cell physiological properties
3. The cell metabolic processes and signaling pathway
4. The cell cycles
5. The structure and function of DNA, RNA and protein
6. The processes of replication, transcription and translation
7. The techniques of molecular biology
8. The interactions between cell and their environment

Assessment: written examination
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Reproductive Medicine and Clinical Embryology
courses.learning_outcomes:
The students are expected to acquire the basic and updated knowledge on:
1. Cell biology and molecular biology of the Cell
2. Modern techniques of cell biology and molecular biology
courses.course_syllabus:
The cell structure and cell organelles
The cell physiological properties
The cell metabolic processes and signaling pathway
The cell cycles
The structure and function of DNA, RNA and protein
The processes of replication, transcription and translation
The techniques of molecular biology
The interactions between cell and their environment

courses.subject: RMCE
courses.title: IVF Laboratory Management and Communication
courses.description:
This course serves to provide basic knowledge and skills on IVF laboratory management, and the communication both inside and outside of the laboratory.
It includes:
1. IVF lab procedure management
2. IVF lab quality control and risk management
3. IVF lab trouble shooting
4. Communication inside of the lab
5. Communication outside of the lab

Assessment: written examination
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Reproductive Medicine and Clinical Embryology
courses.learning_outcomes:
At the conclusion of the course, students should be able to understand:
1. The principle of procedure management
2. The basic requirement of QC/QA system in an IVF lab
3. The principle of risk management
4. The principle of trouble shooting in an IVF lab
5. The principle of communication inside/outside of the lab
courses.course_syllabus:
IVF lab procedure management
IVF lab quality control and risk management
IVF lab trouble shooting
Communication inside of the lab
Communication outside of the lab

courses.subject: RMCE
courses.title: Research Methodology
courses.description:
This course facilitates students to use statistics to carry out their research projects adequately.
It includes:
1. Basic research methodology
2. Basic statistical concepts
3. Research process and encourages an appreciation of why and how research is generated

Assessment: Written Examination
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Reproductive Medicine and Clinical Embryology
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students are expected to be able to:
1. understand basic concepts of research and its methodologies
2. identify the components of a literature review process
3. identify the types of research methods best suited for investigating different types of problems and questions
4. develop research questions that based on and build upon a critical appraisal of existing research
5. understand basic statistical concepts
6. design a research proposal
7. be aware of the ethical principles of research, ethical challenges and approval process
courses.course_syllabus:
Concepts of research
Making a research questions
Screening test and ROC
Introduction to the tool kit: Computer programs and graphics
Probability
Meta-analysis
Prediction
Bayesian Probability
Receiver Operator Characteristics and Exercise
Assessment: what is it about
Course overview, Questions and Answers, evaluation and feedback
Diagnostic testing
Critical appraisal of scientific publications (clinical)
Important great studies in obstetrics
From data collection to laboratory analysis
Common methodologies used in qualitative research
Interventional study, RCT
Ethical and legal issues related to research
Tips to get successful publication
Translating results to practice
To plan a research, what, why
How to write a research proposal
Quality of life assessment
Common data collection methods in quantitative researches and related principles
Screening and cleaning the data
Survey & Questionnaires, gambling
Risk assessment & prediction
Use of Normogram in obstetrics and gynaecology
Literature search
Sources of funding for research and grant writing
Epidemiological study

courses.subject: RMCE
courses.title: IVF Laboratory Design
courses.description:
This course gives students a chance to understand very basic requirement of designing a modern IVF laboratory.
It includes:
1. The basic conception of “clean room”
2. The basic requirement of IVF lab designing
3. The trend of IVF lab design

Assessment: design an IVF lab
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Reproductive Medicine and Clinical Embryology
courses.learning_outcomes:
The students are expected to have knowledge and understand:
1. International requirement for an IVF lab design
2. The difference between a “sterile operation room” and a IVF laboratory
3. The key points of IVF laboratory design
courses.course_syllabus:
The basic conception of “clean room”
The basic requirement of IVF lab designing
The trend of IVF lab design
